title: Windowed stage-1 fNIRS decoder — stop collapsing the hemodynamic trajectory
description: Current fnirs_lda collapses the 22s/220-timepoint window to mean+slope+peak scalars/channel — provably lossy (our feature study: signal is the slope TRAJECTORY, not a global scalar). Build WindowedFnirs: slice window into sub-windows, per-window descriptors, stage-1 classifier per window, aggregate window-probs to block label. The cross-subject ~39% literature ceiling is measured ONLY on the collapsed representation; windowed is UNTESTED cross-subject on Shin. Evaluate within AND cross-subject vs collapse baseline, matched protocol. Composes with domain adaptation (mindscape-lpv) downstream.
close_reason: Built + swept. Keeping time helps within-subject (+2.5pp coarse concat, consistent) but is subject-idiosyncratic (ties collapse cross-subject; fine overfits). MIL pooling dead (smooth HRF, no localized cue). Reframes: cross-subject is distribution shift -> DA (mindscape-lpv) operates on this windowed rep; they compose.

title: Fix fold parallelism — harness runs folds serial (threading backend + GIL)
description: MEASURED (just the bottleneck cell, not full rerun): loky process backend FAILS here — n_jobs=-1 and n_jobs=8 both did 0 folds in 200-400s while workers visibly computed (MNE prints). NOT memory (56GB free). Cause: Windows spawns (not forks) loky workers -> each re-imports the heavy native stack (mne/scipy/mkl), then computes but never returns / respawns. Reverted to threading (only working fold-parallel backend here; GIL-caps GIL-bound FBCSP/CSP/ACM). KEY: sklearn cross_validate(n_jobs>1) uses the SAME loky backend -> mindscape-kvb is blocked by the same env limit. Fold-parallelism is a dead end on this Win+native setup. Working speedups: (1) less per-fold compute = downsample/crop (mindscape-241, changes results, needs re-record); (2) COARSE cell-level OS-process parallelism (independent reproduce_all --only chunks) -> grid floor = slowest single cell (FBCSP nback-cross 979s). No fine-grained fix. Closing as wontfix-in-env.

title: Riemannian baseline (tangent space + MDM) parallel to CSP
description: pyRiemann tangent-space+LR and MDM in baselines/riemann.py, registered in neuroscan.models. Within-subject 0.706 (beats CSP 0.598 and the deep nets); cross-subject 0.357. neuroviz shows per-class Riemann discriminant channel weights under the decoder selector, coloring preserved. Tests: tests/unit/test_baselines_riemann.py. Ref ceilings added.

title: Per-model augmentation policy (don't external-crop nets with internal aug)
description: ATCNet + EEGConformer have built-in sliding-window augmentation; our external 2s crop fights their design (net built for crop_len=500 vs designed ~1125). Policy: crop only nets WITHOUT internal aug (EEGNet/Shallow/Deep); ATCNet/Conformer get full-trial input. Confirmed: Altaheri repo.
close_reason: per-model crop policy implemented; finding: crops actually HELP ATCNet (kept), uncropped regressed

title: Exponential-moving standardization (braindecode-canonical preprocessing)
description: SOTA 2a uses exponential_moving_standardize (factor_new=1e-3, init_block_size=1000), not whole-epoch z-score. Primary-source confirmed (braindecode 2a recipe). Implement as the default standardizer for braindecode decoders. Likely the dominant missing preprocessing piece. See research/deep_dives/2026-06-30_2a_sota_recipe.md
close_reason: continuous-EMS done in braindecode_pre; per-epoch variant in transforms

title: calibrate.py — temperature scaling under shift
description: Port cardiac-seg temp scaling. Fit T on val, report ECE before/after on within vs cross-session/subject. Document in-session calibration does NOT fix cross-session ECE.
close_reason: calibrate.py built + ran (temp scaling, transfer ratio reported)

title: Cross-subject (LOSO) runs — the OOD headline
description: Run --regime cross_subject for csp_lda + atcnet. within->cross gap = the honest contribution + Lys signal. Report gap + calibration shift.
close_reason: cross-subject LOSO ran: 0.382 vs within 0.598 (gap -0.216) = the headline

title: Parity test for ONNX/INT8 quant path
description: Guards the Stage-2 claim. Assert fp32 ONNX logits match torch (<1e-3) and int8 within tolerance, on a tiny synthetic net. No GPU/download.

title: Crop-augmentation decoding (the ATCNet 0.85 lever)
description: Sliding-window crops ~10x training samples. Standard trick behind published ATCNet/ShallowConvNet 2a results. Our undertuned ATCNet (0.567) sits under CSP+LDA (0.598) without it. Add cropped-decoding in decoders.py training.

title: Geometry-aware spatial prior for decoding (inject channel adjacency as knowledge-for-data)
description:
MOTIVATION: our feature vector is flat — it discards the montage geometry. The decoder gets an unordered bag of channels and must re-learn spatial structure from ~700 samples. In a small-data regime, the head layout IS knowledge we're throwing away. Inject it as a prior to compensate for the lack of data (the recurring theme: knowledge > data when data is short).

NOT beamforming: scalp sensors don't 'point' (no propagation/direction like a mic array). The usable geometry is ADJACENCY — neighboring cortex behaves similarly — i.e. a spatial-smoothness prior, not a steering vector.

LADDER (cheap -> expensive):
1. Graph-Laplacian smoothness on channel weights: build a channel graph from mnt positions (edge = proximity), penalize weight differences between neighbors. Cheap, linear, low-risk. START HERE.
2. Spatial basis reduction: represent the channel map in a smooth 2D basis -> far fewer DOF, geometry-aware.
3. ROI pooling: crude discrete version — group channels by anatomical region (e.g. L/R DLPFC, medial PFC).
4. Source localization / DOT: the true beamforming analog (forward model + inverse to cortical sources). Data-hungry, ill-posed for a small montage, needs a head model. Likely overkill; note only.

WHY fNIRS FIRST: EEG is already spatially smeared by volume conduction (neighbors forced similar — smoothing baked in). fNIRS channels are genuinely local/independent, so imposing the CORRECT cortical adjacency is real information, not redundant. Locality is fNIRS's one edge; a geometry prior is the tool that finally uses it. (Contrast: CSP for EEG is a DATA-DRIVEN spatial filter — learns, needs samples; the geometry prior is imposed/free/small-data-friendly. Complementary.)

HONEST — UNKNOWN IF IT HELPS: this denoises + may transfer better cross-subject; it does NOT invent signal (if the signal is genuinely weak, e.g. n-back level, expect little). Over-smoothing can blur a focal signal on a small montage — must tune the penalty, not force it. Prove/refute empirically: geometry-prior decoder vs flat baseline, cross-subject, matched protocol.

WHY IT'S ON THE ROADMAP REGARDLESS (the pyramid): the common base under every task/modality we do is ONE brain-signal decoding model. A geometry-aware spatial prior is a foundational, reusable capability for that base — it serves MI, workload, EEG, fNIRS, fusion alike. High structural value for the portfolio pyramid even if the per-task accuracy gain turns out modest.

NEEDS: mnt_nback.mat channel positions (we have them). Prototype on fNIRS Shin n-back first.

title: Marker-back the fNIRS recipe CV table (kill README magic constants)
description: PR #8 review: recipe acc/kappa table in README is hand-typed magic constants. Have recipes.py write runs/<recipe>_cross_subject_shin2017_nback/aggregate.json (harness schema) + results.record(), rebuild results.json, wrap the table cells in sync_numbers markers.

title: Refactor fNIRS feature studies into feature_importance/ subpackage + consolidate shared CV
description: PR #8 review: (1) folder-separate optuna_fnirs/fnirs_subset_select/fnirs_recipes into neuroscan/tasks/workload/feature_importance/ with better separation; (2) consolidate the duplicated subject-grouped StratifiedGroupKFold fold loop shared by optuna+recipes into a shared _cv helper. Update tests, README links, config paths.

title: fNIRS subset+aggregation study: softmax-simplex weights, entropy sparsity, Pareto, F vs D aggregation
description: Refined design (user): continuous logits -> softmax simplex weights (magnitude decoupled from selection; standardize-then-weight already handles raw-scale fairness). Multi-objective NSGA-II: maximize CV acc, minimize effective-#-features exp(entropy(w)) (scale-free sparsity). Two aggregation modes compared: F=weighted-concat->LDA, D=per-family LDA->weighted vote (simplex = valid mixture; D hosts the orthogonal-panel idea). Pareto front acc vs eff-#-features; knee = minimal fair subset; honest held-out validation of the knee (seal 20% subjects, search on 80%). Stability across seeds. Iterative. Part of epic mindscape-b9g.

title: Optuna fNIRS study: weighted per-family search + importance readout (step 3)
description: neuroscan/tuning/optuna_fnirs.py: search w_family in [0,1] per family; objective = mean acc of scaler->weight->shrink-LDA over repeated-seeded 5-fold GroupKFold (store + harness.folds_for + metrics). Output: get_param_importances (fANOVA) + top-K trial weight distribution + seed-stability across 2-3 reruns. Search space + n_trials in a small YAML (config-over-argv). Add optuna to pyproject. Depends on the feature-bank task.

title: fNIRS feature bank + assemble fn in core/features (step 1+2)
description: core/features: FNIRS_FEATURES registry {name: fn(X[n,72,220])->[n,72]} of per-channel temporal descriptors — mean, slope, peak (have), + variance, skew, kurtosis, AUC, time-to-peak, min, range, early-slope, late-slope, HbO-HbR corr. GLM-beta as a follow-up (needs HRF regressor). extract-all-once + cache; assemble(blocks, weights) builds StandardScaler->per-family-weight->matrix (weight AFTER scaling — before cancels). Pure fns, tests per descriptor. Keeps core=extract, baselines=call layering.

title: fNIRS feature-importance study via weighted-feature Optuna search
description: Epic done: importance study (wgk, slope #1) + differentiable subset study (5x4, slope survives sparsity, per-channel unidentifiable) + honest fixed-recipe CV (dynamics ties full bank, slope-trajectory carries it, mean/peak weak). Documented in README Shin-task section. Convergent finding: fNIRS workload = hemodynamic slope trajectory, not amplitude.

title: Add classical EEG methods MDM, FgMDM, FBCSP + baseline them on both tasks
description: Register riemann_mdm (class exists), add riemann_fgmdm (pyriemann FgMDM) + fbcsp (filter-bank CSP + MI select + LDA). Thread fs through get_method for filter design. Run within+cross on BCI-2a and nback_eeg, record, compare vs our riemann/csp + SOTA. Expectation from theory: FgMDM ~= TS+LR, FBCSP <= riemann, MDM floor.

title: Improve fNIRS to be competitive in fusion (CORAL/OT DA + feature bank)
description: Re-centering made EEG the strong workload modality (0.45->0.60); fNIRS stuck ~0.47, so fusion is strong+weak = a wash. To get a real fusion WIN, fNIRS needs to close the gap. Try: (1) cross-subject domain adaptation for fNIRS (CORAL feature-alignment unsupervised, or optimal-transport/GW per the deep-dive ~+10-15pp on Tufts), (2) richer feature bank (AUC/time-to-peak/HbT/higher moments) — deep-dive says marginal but verify on our data, (3) GLM/HRF beta features. Goal: fNIRS ~0.55+ so EEG+fNIRS are a strong+strong pair where complementarity (oracle 0.75) can actually be cashed.

title: Reorg: feature extraction -> core/, baselines/ thin + grouped (eeg/fnirs/fusion)
description: Vision (user): the substance is FEATURE EXTRACTION (covariances/time-delay, recenter, band-power, fnirs amplitude, dispersion-scale) = core/ infrastructure; baselines/ methods are THIN 'call core + classify' wrappers, grouped by modality eeg/fnirs/fusion. NOT unet-in-systole where the model carries its own infra. Phases: (1) core/features.py = extractors, callers import it. (2) regroup baselines -> eeg/fnirs/fusion subpackages, thin objects. (3) extract transfer (recenter/rescale/RPA/MDWM) from align.py entrypoint into baselines/eeg/transfer.py; align thin. (4) extract fusion method logic into baselines/fusion/; run_fusion thin. Test+commit per phase; update registry+imports+tests throughout.
notes: Phases 1-3 DONE: (1) core/features/ package by family fce2d84+split; (2) baselines/eeg+fnirs grouped thin d0d72a7; (3) transfer -> baselines/eeg/transfer.py 9e084bf. All behavior-preserved, 91 tests. Phase 4 (fusion combiners late/feature/gate + aggregators -> baselines/fusion/, run_fusion/fusion_gate thin) REMAINS — the tangled one (sweep+complementarity intertwined w/ fold loop); deferred pending user call.
close_reason: All 4 phases done: core/features/ package; baselines/eeg+fnirs+fusion grouped+thin; transfer -> baselines/eeg/transfer.py; fusion combiners -> baselines/fusion/. Behavior-preserved throughout, 93 tests. Entrypoints (align, run_fusion, fusion_gate) now thin runners.

title: Finish the RPA transfer ladder (zero-shot re-scale; calibrated RPA + MDWM)
description:
Re-centering (TLCenter) was step 1 of Riemannian Procrustes Analysis. Finish it, reported BY REGIME:
ZERO-SHOT (no target labels, deployment-real): re-center (have, 0.501) -> + re-scale (TLScale). Does dispersion-alignment add over location-alignment?
CALIBRATED (a FEW target labels): full RPA (TLCenter+TLScale+TLRotate) + MDWM (min-dist weighted mean).
HARD CONSTRAINT (leak): the calibrated target labels MUST come from a DISJOINT calibration split of the held-out subject, NEVER the test blocks. Protocol: carve a labeled calib-slice (stratified, seeded) from the target subject, fit rotation/MDWM on source+calib, evaluate on the REMAINING (disjoint) target blocks only. Report calib fraction. Mirrors the honest calibration-half used for per-subject z-score.
Deliver: methods in the transfer path (pyriemann.transfer: TLCenter/TLScale/TLRotate/MDWM), BCI-2a cross-subject run, README transfer table extended with the zero-shot vs calibrated split, tests. Then EEG done; fNIRS DA (CORAL) next.
close_reason: Done in PR #6. RPA ladder on BCI-2a cross-subject: zero-shot recenter 0.501/+rescale 0.519; calibrated RPA 0.555/0.595/0.650 (10/20/50%), MDWM 0.412. Leak-free disjoint calib split. Marker-synced, tested (89).

title: Integrate per-subject unsupervised calibration as an EEG-workload transfer regime
description: Per-subject z-scoring lifts EEG-bandpower workload 0.407->~0.51 (honest calib-half)/0.581 (transductive) cross-subject - a real transfer fix (MI re-centering analog). Needs proper eval integration: subject IDs plumbed to a feature-normalization step, a 'cross_subject_calibrated' regime or a calibration-set protocol (NOT transductive - use a held-out unlabeled calibration slice per test subject). Then: corrected Experiment-2 numbers (EEG becomes strong modality), recompute oracle/complementarity on z-features (0.766), and a Table-B calibrated column. Report calib-half as headline (defensible), transductive as upper bound.

title: Learned per-trial fusion to capture EEG-fNIRS complementarity
description: Complementarity PROVEN: oracle (either modality correct) 0.688 vs best-single 0.474 = +21pt headroom; error-corr phi=0.05 (near-independent); EEG-only-right 21.5%, fNIRS-only-right 25.5%, both-wrong 31%. Naive late/feature fusion captures NONE (0.468 ~ best single). Target: a per-trial GATE / compact cross-attention that picks/weights the trusted modality per block, closing the 0.468->0.688 gap (even partial = real gain). CONSTRAINTS (from SOTA research 2026-07-01_eeg_fnirs_fusion_sota.md): n=26/~700 blocks, so compact only (shallow per-modality encoders + ONE cross-attn or a learned gate, d_model<=32, dropout>=0.5), strict NESTED GroupKFold, per-subject standardization; gate any reported gain on beating fNIRS-alone on the SAME outer folds with CIs excluding 0, else it's the DC-AGIN overfit collapse. Oracle is an upper bound, not a promise.
notes: GATE RESULT: compact input-gate model (per-modality encoders + per-trial gate, nested GroupKFold, per-subject z-score, dropout 0.5) = 0.573 fold-mean. NOT a fusion win: it equals z-scored-EEG-alone (0.581) and z-concat-LDA (0.578); the gate adds nothing over best-single. The +0.099-vs-fNIRS was per-subject z-scoring rescuing EEG, caught by assume-wrong ablation, NOT fusion. Late fusion with z-features 0.575 also ties best-single. Oracle with z-features 0.766 (+0.185) - headroom even bigger but still uncaptured. CONCLUSION: learned output-mixing gate also fails; confidence-independent per-trial reliability still not learned. Keep fusion_gate.py as an honest negative artifact.

title: Reproduce BenchNIRS fNIRS baseline under matched grouped-CV eval
description: Our fnirs_lda 0.442 (LOSO, shrinkage-LDA) is NOT comparable to BenchNIRS's 0.389 (Lyu 2023, Frontiers Neuroergonomics 4:994969) -- different eval (5-fold GROUP CV + nested inner-3-fold tuning vs our LOSO), classifier (plain vs shrinkage LDA), features/window. So 'we beat them' is invalid. Fix: implement BenchNIRS's exact protocol as a matched baseline in our harness and reproduce ~38.9% LDA on Shin n-back (3-class, chance 33.3%), verifying our pipeline correctness. Then our method head-to-head under the SAME eval. Verified from primary source: BenchNIRS LDA 38.9%, SVC 35.0%, kNN 31.6%, ANN 32.5%, CNN 39.3% (p=0.037), LSTM 34.4% -- most near/below chance; their contribution IS exposing that published fNIRS accuracies are inflated by improper (personalised/within-session) validation. Implement efficient: vectorized features, joblib-parallel folds, GPU for the DL baselines if reproduced. Repo: github BenchNIRS (HanBnrd).

title: unify Baseline + decoders under one Decoder interface (reuse)
description: Baseline only covered classical methods; BraindecodeClf has the same shape (.fit->self, .predict_proba) under a different probs-method name (.score vs .predict_proba). Extract a single Decoder Protocol (neuroscan/models/base.py) that BOTH baselines and braindecode nets satisfy structurally. Rename Baseline.score -> predict_proba (align sklearn + nets; sklearn .score=accuracy is a footgun). Unify get_method: one score adapter (clf.predict_proba) for baselines AND nets; per-name builder for fit. Reuse + organization, per PR#2 review follow-up. Epic-scoped mindscape-snf.

title: baselines: Baseline interface + method classes (TangentSpace/Mdm/Acm/CspLda/FnirsLda)
description: Add baselines/base.py Baseline ABC (.fit(X,y)->self, .score(X)->probs[n,C]). Rewrite riemann/csp_lda/fnirs_features as classes with hyperparams as __init__ args: TangentSpace(estimator), Mdm(estimator), Acm(order,lag,estimator), CspLda(n_components), FnirsLda(). Replaces the riemann dict-dispatch with real impls. Keep module-level fit/score shims for back-compat (neuroviz, tests). Update models.get_method to build object-backed (fit_fn, score_fn); score_fn becomes uniform clf.score(X). Harness unchanged. Verify identical output. Review comment riemann.py:77. Epic-scoped.

title: Architecture: method objects (interface+impl, hyperparams as init args)
description: PR#2 review lesson + systole reference: replace loose function-pairs + if/elif/dict-dispatch with proper interface + implementation objects that own their hyperparams (init args) and encompass their logic. systole pattern: config lives WITH the class it configures (pydantic-validated), objects injected. mindscape already does this for DATA (EpochCfg/FnirsCfg); the gap is the METHOD side (baselines are module fit/score fns + kwargs + dict-dispatch). Keep the harness (fit_fn,score_fn) contract + back-compat module shims so the ~12 call sites migrate safely. Behavior identical, verified. Work on dev.
close_reason: Both subtasks done: baseline method objects (2s2 -- Baseline interface + TangentSpace/Mdm/Acm/CspLda/FnirsLda, hyperparams as init args, dict-dispatch gone) and TemperatureScaler (eg3). Harness contract preserved via get_method._adapt + back-compat module shims; systole's config-lives-with-class pattern followed. Suite 67 pass / integration green.

title: fNIRS Stage 3: pull EEG n-back half (same-task EEG-vs-fNIRS comparison)
description: Shin set is hybrid — VPxxx-EEG.zip has EEG n-back on the SAME subjects/task. Pull it, add an EEG-nback adapter, decode with EEG methods -> EEG-vs-fNIRS on n-back (same task, no confound) = the real modality comparison + fusion basis. Goes in the n-back workload table (Table B), NOT motor imagery.
notes:
PLAN + findings (2026-07-01):
DATA: n-back EEG NOT in MOABB (Shin2017A/B = MI + mental-arith, different tasks). Comes from same DepositOnce source as our fNIRS: EEG_01-26_MATLAB.zip (6.09GB, BBCI .mat) from doc.ml.tu-berlin.de/simultaneous_EEG_NIRS. Downloading now -> <data>/raw/shin2017_eeg_matlab.zip. Ref GitHub JaeyoungShin/simultaneous_EEG-NIRS for exact cnt/mrk fields.
EEG SPECS: 22 channels (10 frontal, 12 parieto-occipital), BBCI cnt(.x [T,ch], .fs, .clab) + mrk(.time ms, .y one-hot, .className) mirroring NIRS. 0/2/3-back load = 3-class -> shares CANONICAL_NBACK, goes in Table B (n-back workload), NOT motor imagery.
ADAPTER: core/data/eeg/shin2017_nback_eeg.py, Shin2017NbackEegAdapter mirroring fnirs/shin2017.py but cnt.x (not oxy/deoxy) + EpochCfg. Discover subjects by globbing **/cnt_nback.mat (naming-robust). Register 'shin2017_nback_eeg'.
DECODE: csp_lda + riemann (unlike fNIRS, EEG workload band-power IS covariance-visible: frontal theta / parietal alpha) -> EEG-vs-fNIRS same-task comparison in Table B. Confirm epoch window + zip dir naming from real data at build time.
STATUS: waiting on 6GB download to complete, then inspect .mat + build + decode.

title: Stage 3: hybrid EEG-fNIRS fusion (complementarity grid)
description: After fNIRS-alone + EEG-alone exist on the same subjects (Shin 2017): fuse. Feature-level and/or decision-level fusion; handle EEG-ms vs fNIRS-seconds temporal alignment (hemodynamic lag). The claim = fusion beats either modality alone on the complementarity grid (EEG strong MI, fNIRS strong arithmetic). Depends on Stage 1 (EEG) + Stage 2 (fNIRS).
notes: OUTPUT-SPACE FUSION FULLY CLOSED. Swept mean/product/conf-weight/maxconf-pick/stacking(meta-LDA nested CV)/cal-mean/cal-conf-weight -> ALL <= best-single 0.474 (best 0.469). Root cause MEASURED: confidence !predict correctness (conf-gap eeg +0.023, fnirs +0.038; ~0), calibration doesnt fix ordering only scale. => reliability signal NOT in probabilities => only an INPUT-reading gate (learned cross-modal attention) can reach oracle 0.688. run_fusion.py now records the full sweep+conf-gap (marker-synced). Child task = the input-gate model.

title: fNIRS: core/data/fnirs/ adapter (MBLL + motion + bandpass)
description: Parse Shin 2017 fNIRS DIRECT from TU Berlin .mat (MOABB loads EEG only). Chain: optical_density -> scalp_coupling_index -> TDDR -> beer_lambert_law(ppf) -> filter(0.05,0.7) -> epoch HbO/HbR [n,ch,t]. Emit mindscape schema so it feeds the harness. Add mne-nirs/mne dep. Verify ppf default per MNE version at build.
close_reason: Built: core/data/fnirs/{base,shin2017}.py parses HbO/HbR from Shin .mat, feeds harness. fnirs_lda (mean+slope+peak->LDA) 0.442 LOSO / 0.415 within (3-class n-back, chance 0.333). CSP/Riemann at chance (wrong tool). run_fnirs CLI + tests. Committed+pushed.

title: fNIRS: research Shin-2018 loader + workload recipe + post-2018 datasets
description: Lock: exact Shin 2018 contents + how to load (mne-nirs/MOABB/raw), canonical fNIRS preprocessing (OD->MBLL->TDDR->bandpass->HbO/HbR) with params, decoding SOTA numbers (workload + MI, within + cross-subject), short-separation/systemic-physiology handling, and any bigger/newer open fNIRS or hybrid datasets beyond 2018 (Tufts fNIRS2MW etc). Deliverable: research/deep_dives/ file.
close_reason: Research done: research/deep_dives/2026-07-01_fnirs_landscape.md. Key: Shin 2017 (not 2018) = both tasks; MOABB fNIRS not implemented (parse .mat); mne chain confirmed; Riemann-on-fNIRS validated (Naher 2025); Tufts fNIRS2MW for scale.

title: Stage 2: fNIRS + hybrid EEG-fNIRS modality (parallel)
description: Add the second non-invasive dimension: fNIRS (hemodynamic). Task = mental workload/arithmetic (fNIRS home turf, prefrontal). TARGET = SHIN 2017 (TNSRE), 29 subj, has BOTH left/right MI + mental arithmetic, EEG(30ch@1000Hz)+fNIRS(36ch@~12.5Hz), GPL-3.0 -> complementarity grid (EEG strong MI, fNIRS strong arithmetic, fusion beats either). Plan: fNIRS-alone arithmetic FIRST, then EEG-alone (same subjects, harness reuse), then fusion. BUILD CONSTRAINT: MOABB Shin2017A/B loads EEG only (fNIRS NotImplementedError) -> must parse HbO/HbR direct from TU Berlin .mat. Preprocessing (mne.preprocessing.nirs): optical_density -> scalp_coupling_index -> TDDR -> beer_lambert_law(ppf, version-dependent!) -> filter(0.05,0.7). Riemann/recentering spine applies (Naher 2025 HybridBlocks validated on fNIRS cov). Honest-eval: systemic-physiology leakage — but Shin has NO short-separation channels, so note as stated limitation. See research/deep_dives/2026-07-01_fnirs_landscape.md.
close_reason: fNIRS-alone vertical shipped: Shin adapter, fnirs_lda 0.442 LOSO / 0.415 within (covariance-methods-fail finding), neuroviz both-modality + predictions/score, M5 curriculum, 3 deep-dives. Hybrid remainder (EEG n-back half + fusion) tracked by mindscape-f8f + mindscape-lpv (Stage 3).

title: Riemannian manifold re-centering for cross-subject transfer
description: Plain tangent-space Riemann cross-subject = 0.357, no better than CSP (0.382). The win needs manifold re-centering / Riemannian alignment (recenter each subject's covariances to a common reference SPD point before tangent mapping) — pyriemann transfer-learning (TLCenter/RPA). This is the principled fix for the -0.108..-0.24 cross-subject gap. Target ~0.55-0.60 LOSO.
close_reason: Implemented Riemannian re-centering (Zanini 2018) in baselines/riemann.recenter_covariances + experiments/align.py. Cross-subject LOSO 0.357 -> 0.496 (+0.139). Parallelized over folds (joblib). Full RPA (rotation+stretch) remains as optional further work.

title: neuroviz: responsive full-screen layout
description: Fixed-width panels don't fill the viewport; wasted space. Make it a responsive grid (topomap + full-width waveforms), canvases sized to container with resize handling.
close_reason: responsive grid fills viewport, canvases size to container + resize handler

title: neuroviz: show all 22 channels in waveforms (not just C3/Cz/C4)
description: Current waveform panel shows only the motor trio for readability. The general/correct view is all 22 channels (stacked small-multiples), motor trio highlighted. Export all channels; render stacked.
close_reason: all 22 channels exported + rendered stacked, motor trio highlighted

title: MOABB Windows drive-colon bug — _sanitize_path strips D: -> D- (data leaks to repo)
description: moabb/datasets/download.py _sanitize_path translates ':' over whole path incl drive colon, making downloads relative -> leak into repo cwd as D-/ + re-download every time. Worked around via monkeypatch in core.config._patch_moabb_drive_colon (preserves drive, sanitizes rest). Upstream bug; consider PR. Root cause confirmed by source read.
close_reason: patched (preserve drive) + scoped to native Windows; WSL is zero-patch native path

title: Seed-averaging for reportable mean+/-std
description: SOTA averages 10 runs; we run 1 seed -> collapse subjects drag the mean. Add N-seed averaging per fold (already a P3 bead fdn; bump rationale: it's a fairness fix not just hygiene).

title: Public README (the flip artifact)
description: README skeleton from siblings: one-line -> honest question -> 3 pieces -> the number that matters -> where-it-fails -> data -> quickstart -> tests -> license. The public-flip trigger at Stage 0.
close_reason: README updated with real Stage-0 results + honest limits

title: Stage 3: THINGS-EEG2 semantic decoding (harder task)
description: BIDS adapter, same canonical schema + harness. Visual/semantic decoding = Lys comms-thesis target. Cross-subject + calibration the headline.

title: Model cards per run
description: Auto card at run-end: method, recipe, regime, metrics, vs-reference, where-it-fails (worst subject). cardiac-seg modelcard.py pattern.

title: EEGNet trains to chance on some subjects
description: EEGNet subj1 hit 0.257 (chance) in quantize run while ATCNet trained fine. Hparam/instability. Check after crop-aug (crops may fix). Sweep lr/epochs for eegnet specifically.
close_reason: fixed by the proper recipe (z-score, full window): EEGNet reproduction has no chance-collapse subjects (worst 0.42)

title: INT8 dynamic quant degenerate on tiny EEG nets
description: EEGNet 26KB: int8 bigger+slower (overhead nodes > savings) + onnx version_converter RuntimeError. Decide: drop int8 default, report fp32 edge size+latency as the honest 'already edge-tiny' story; keep int8 optional/static.
close_reason: Moot — the project is effectively classical (CSP/Riemann/LDA), which is already trivially tiny (a few matrices); nothing to quantize. INT8 was net-specific (EEGNet/ATCNet) and adds overhead at 26KB scale anyway. Edge-deploy is a non-goal, not a bug. Not fixing the onnx version_converter int8 path.

title: Subject-5 below-chance collapse (acc 0.215, kappa -0.046)
description: PROGRESS: principled fixes (continuous data + proper 1.5-6s window + z-score/StandardScaler not EMS + batch64 + arch confirmed matching Altaheri) lifted ATCNet within-subject 0.567->0.619 (runs/reproduce_zscore_full). Hard subjects s2/5/6 recovered ~0.40->0.42-0.49. Residual ~0.19 vs published 0.81 = single-run vs their 10-run-mean + Adam-vs-AdamW + callbacks. Diminishing returns; method reproduced, peak not chased (per plan honesty rule). Clean subjects match (s3 0.79). Consider closing.
close_reason: Not a bug — honest hard-subject result, already recovered. The 0.215 below-chance was pre-fix; current within runs put subject-5 at ATCNet 0.309 / Riemann 0.465 / CSP 0.337, all above 0.25 chance. Principled preprocessing (continuous data + 1.5-6s window + z-score) resolved the collapse. Residual low s5 = single-run vs published 10-run-mean, peak not chased per honesty rule.

title: Tests for decoders / export_onnx / quantize
description: New GPU+quant code untested. decoders fit smoke (cpu tiny), export_onnx parity, quantize triad shape.

title: calibrate.py — temperature scaling + cross-session-limited finding
description: Port cardiac-seg temp scaling. Fit T on val, report ECE before/after on within vs cross-session test. Document: in-session calibration does NOT fix cross-session ECE (already observed: ATCNet ECE 0.31 survived early-stop).

title: reference.yaml + reference.py — published SOTA ceilings
description: Cited literature ceilings (FBCSP ~0.57k, EEGNet ~0.70, ATCNet ~0.85, transformer SOTA ~0.88) as config to quarantine against, printed next to our results. cardiac-seg pattern. Honesty rule: cite, do not chase.
close_reason: reference.yaml + reference.py done, wired into run output

title: Persist Optuna fNIRS study to JournalStorage + expand diagnostics
description: PR #8 review: use the optuna database (JournalStorage under runs/) so trials are reproducible+queryable; expand written diagnostics + note importance table is generated from the committed study artifact.

title: fNIRS GLM-beta feature (HRF-convolved regressor betas)
description: The fNIRS-native statistical feature: per-channel OLS fit of an HRF-convolved boxcar regressor over the block window -> beta (amplitude) + t-stat as features. Add to FNIRS_FEATURES once the bank + study exist. More principled than raw mean (scores vs the canonical hemodynamic shape).

title: Add per-cell wall-time logging to reproduce_all
description: Currently reverse-engineer timing from aggregate.json mtimes. Print elapsed per cell (time.perf_counter around each harness.run) so grid runs report timings directly.

title: Code analytics tool — LOC + complexity distribution
description: Promote the ad-hoc LOC/complexity script into a committed repo tool (e.g. tools/analytics.py or a make/uv target): per-file + per-area code lines, def count, and a branch-node cyclomatic proxy (if/for/while/try/bool/comprehension), src-vs-test ratio, top-N biggest files. Useful for tracking that methods stay thin + complexity stays in the engine as the codebase grows. Consider: real cyclomatic (radon), a threshold gate (flag files over N code / branch), and a distribution snapshot in docs. Seeded by the one-off run in this session (methods near-branchless, logic in evaluation/core.data).

title: verify + benchmark pyriemann torch/GPU backend (0.11)
description: KNOWN-safe first step. Confirm pyriemann 0.11 accepts CUDA torch tensors in the geometry ops (mean_riemann, tangent_space, covariance) -> runs on GPU AND matches the numpy result (parity). Benchmark GPU vs CPU on our covariance sizes ([22,22] bnci, [72,72] fnirs, [28,28] eeg) x fold counts. Decide from the measurement whether GPU is worth wiring in. No pipeline changes yet. Umbrella mindscape-r9b.
close_reason: Measured: pyriemann numpy-only (no GPU path), and Riemann ops are already 36-400ms at our 22-28 dim sizes = not a bottleneck. GPU offers no win. No pipeline change.

title: Perf: measure-first, conservative GPU/opt for classical baselines (umbrella)
description: Umbrella. PRINCIPLE (user): do NOT rewrite before we know we must, and be conservative even then -- prefer using a lib's OWN features over reimplementing. We use several libs (MNE=CSP, pyriemann=Riemann, sklearn=LDA, braindecode=nets); only pyriemann is confirmed to have a GPU path (Array API / torch tensors). Measure whether GPU is even needed first (parallel folds already got runs to ~20-30s). Children: verify+bench pyriemann torch backend; audit MNE CSP options; only then, IF worth it, wire GPU via the lib feature, parity-gated.
notes: MEASURE-FIRST RESULT: (1) pyriemann=numpy-only, no GPU path [mindscape-tos closed]. (2) At our matrix sizes (22-28 dim) Riemann ops are 36-400ms = not bottleneck. (3) Cold-import + sequential-fold overhead already fixed by reproduce_all (1 process) + threading fold parallelism. Net: no classical-baseline rewrite justified. Remaining open lever = mindscape-oje (MNE CSP docs audit, P4, low pri). Conservative conclusion: STOP here unless a real bottleneck reappears under profiling.

title: STRUCTURE.md: embed mermaid architecture diagrams (after PR#2 merge)
description: Add visual overview to docs/STRUCTURE.md (NOT a separate architecture/ folder -- avoid diagram<->prose drift, same reason we killed README/results drift). Mermaid (GitHub renders inline; not plantuml). Keep coarse (layer/contract level, ages well): (1) system overview graph -- core (engine + Decoder contract) <- baselines + neuroscan/models (implement) -> harness (consumes); data flow signal->preprocess->decode->eval; (2) class diagram for the contract arc -- Decoder <| Baseline <| {TangentSpace,Mdm,Acm,CspLda,FnirsLda} + BraindecodeClf |> Decoder. Do AFTER PR#2 merges (architecture just settled; don't bloat the PR).

title: calibrate: extract a TemperatureScaler object
description: calibrate.py temperature-scaling logic (fit T on val, apply, summarize) -> a small object that owns T + the fit/apply, instead of loose functions + inline summary. Review comment calibrate.py:94. Epic-scoped.

title: harden tests for vectorized crops + fNIRS epoching
description: Pass-2 vectorizations (2kl) pass existing tests, but crops multi-crop values and epoch_blocks sample-extraction are only asserted at shape/count/constant-signal level -- a subtle indexing/transpose regression could slip. Add value-level assertions (crop windows match source slices + tidx order; epoch extracts correct samples via a ramp signal + baseline). Locks in the vectorized behavior.

title: vectorize data-path loops: crops + fNIRS epoching
description: transforms.crops builds chunk/tidx lists per start -> fancy-index all starts at once (idx=starts[:,None]+arange(crop_len); X[:,:,idx] transpose+reshape; tidx=tile(arange(N),n_crops)). fnirs/base.epoch_blocks loops onsets with a drop-if-offedge branch -> precompute valid mask, fancy-index segments, vectorize baseline subtraction. Both correctness-sensitive (tests exist) -- verify equivalence. Pass-2 epic.

title: metrics: vectorize confusion matrix (bincount)
description: confusion() accumulates with a Python zip loop (cm[t,p]+=1). Replace with np.bincount(t*n_classes+p, minlength=n*n).reshape(n,n). Hot-ish (called per eval). Verify identical output. Pass-2 epic.

title: Perf/density pass 2 (rescan follow-ups)
description: Second-pass scan after the mindscape-02r cleanup. Thinner batch (codebase mostly clean now): vectorize the confusion-matrix loop, the crops augmentation, and the fNIRS epoching loop; drop a leftover inner function in calibrate. Harness fold_mean left again (idiomatic). Work on dev, one subtask per commit, verify equivalence.
close_reason: All 3 subtasks done: confusion->bincount (lpg), crops+fnirs-epoching vectorized (2kl), calibrate inner-fn dropped (fje). Second pass confirms codebase now largely clean -- no dead code, remaining loops correct-by-design.

title: densify: harness fold loop + neuroviz per-class lookups
description: harness.py fold loop uses parallel append lists (per/P/Y/G) + scattered accumulation -> tighten. neuroviz export.py/export_fnirs.py use np.where(y==c)[0][0] for scalar first-index lookups (O(n) overkill) and call y.tolist() repeatedly -> cache sorted(np.unique(y)) once, use argmax(y==c) or a prebuilt index. Cosmetic clarity, no behavior change. Part of epic mindscape-02r.

title: drop if/elif chains: riemann method + decoders crop branch
description: riemann.py fit() has a 3-branch if/elif on method ('ts'/'mdm'/'acm') -> dict-dispatch of pipeline builders. decoders.py has a nested if(cl)/if(use_val) block with duplicated use_val logic -> guard-clause / helper. Matches the review theme (denser, less branching). Part of epic mindscape-02r.

title: neuroviz export: vectorize frame binning + dedup mu/beta filtering
description: export.py + export_fnirs.py build topomap frames with a per-frame Python slice-mean loop ([erd[:,edges[i]:edges[i+1]].mean(1) for i in range(n_frames)]) -- replace with a single vectorized binned mean (reshape or np.add.reduceat). export.py also calls _erd_frames twice (mu, beta), re-filtering the 22ch signal each time; factor the filter so the band power is computed without redundant filtering passes. Part of epic mindscape-02r.

title: baselines: trim redundant float64 casts + cache fNIRS feature constants
description: riemann.py/csp_lda.py cast X.astype(float64) on BOTH fit and score every fold (pyriemann/MNE need f64 internally, but the double-cast + per-call churn is avoidable). fnirs_features._features recomputes tc and (tc**2).sum() every call (constants) and casts the small feature matrix to float64 (LDA is fine with float32). Cache the time-axis constants; drop the unnecessary f64 promotion on fNIRS features. Part of epic mindscape-02r.

title: Code densification + resource optimization pass
description: Quality-first sweep from a codebase scan: densify verbose blocks, replace if/elif chains with dispatch/guard-clauses, vectorize per-item numpy loops, trim redundant dtype casts + recomputation, and audit GPU/device usage. Honest scope: this is a small-data research repo (22ch x 1125 samples, ~288 trials, 26 subjects) so raw speed gains are marginal -- the win is clarity + correctness + removing waste, not a benchmark number. GPU paths already look correct (explicit device + autocast); audit, don't invent work. Work on dev branch, one subtask per commit.
close_reason: All 5 subtasks done: fnirs feature caching+f32 (5yy), reduceat frame-binning (9yp), riemann dict-dispatch + decoders split (8qe), neuroviz argmax/unique densify (gi5), GPU/parallelism audit verified no-op (n81). Honest outcome: mostly clarity/correctness wins; GPU already optimal; a couple scan suggestions were misreads (mu/beta filters not redundant; csp/riemann f64 casts necessary) and correctly left alone.

title: neuroviz: ONNX decode-viewer (in-browser model inference, cardioview-style)
description: DEFERRED - polish the signal-viz first, decide later. Add a Decode mode: ONNX model selector (EEGNet/ATCNet) running in-browser (onnxruntime-web) on a held-out trial -> predicted class + 4 softmax bars + correct/wrong. The PLAN's decode-viewer piece, parallels cardioview. Keep Signal + Decode as two modes.

title: Seed averaging for stable numbers
description: Single-seed now. Average N seeds per fold for reportable mean+std (publishable honesty). Cheap on 5090.

title: learning/ track — neuroscience + decoding theory
description: Mirror siblings: learning/<date>_<topic>.md + glossary + quizzes. Ramp artifacts: MI/CSP, EEGNet/ATCNet, calibration, MEG vs EEG.
close_reason: learning/ crash-course track built (3 lessons + field-map + glossary + quizzes), research-grounded

title: Optional: efficient edge deploy (method-dependent, not a stage)
description: fp32 ONNX size+latency across methods, parity-gated. Honest 'already tiny' framing + optional static int8. Tie to Lys bandwidth angle.

title: Backfill/normalize early runs into mlflow
description: ATCNet no-crop + crop runs wrote single .json (old run.py). Convert to runs/<name>/ dirs or re-run; run tracking.backfill so UI has full history.

title: Model cards per run
description: Auto model card (method, recipe, regime, metrics, where-it-fails) at run-end. cardiac-seg modelcard.py pattern.

title: Make baselines sklearn estimators + route folds through cross_validate
description: BLOCKED by env: sklearn cross_validate parallel folds use joblib loky (process) backend, which fails on this Windows+native-stack setup exactly like the hand-rolled loky attempt (see mindscape-07n) — workers spawn, re-import mne/scipy/mkl, never return. So routing folds through cross_validate would NOT gain parallelism here. Only value left = sklearn-estimator compatibility (pipelines/GridSearch), not speed. Deprioritize.

title: Downsample nback recipe 100->60Hz for eval speed
description: 4-30Hz needs ~64Hz Nyquist; running 100Hz. ~1.6x fewer samples on every nback cell, faithful. One recipe change in experiments.yaml nback_eeg_* entries. Revalidate numbers unchanged-ish.

title: audit MNE CSP for GPU / faster-covariance options (docs, don't assume)
description: MNE CSP .fit = 4.5s/fold (the measured bottleneck, ledoit_wolf shrinkage). Do NOT assume it's numpy-only -- check MNE docs for cupy/GPU paths, a faster covariance estimator, or a rank/reg option that cuts the cost. Also: is ledoit_wolf worth it vs a cheaper estimator (measure accuracy delta)? Report findings; change nothing without measurement. Umbrella mindscape-r9b.

title: calibrate: drop inner mean() helper
description: calibrate summary uses an inner mean(k) closure over rows, called 5x. Precompute means = {k: float(np.mean([r[k] for r in rows])) for k in keys} and reference it -- drops the inner function (matches earlier review preference). Cosmetic. Pass-2 epic.

title: GPU/device + parallelism audit (honest, likely mostly no-op)
description: Verify decoders.py device selection + autocast are optimal; confirm no stray CPU tensors on the train/infer path; check align.py joblib n_jobs default is sane for LOSO folds. Scan says GPU is already correct -- this task is to CONFIRM and note findings, not manufacture changes. Close as verified if nothing to do. Part of epic mindscape-02r.
close_reason: Audited, no change: GPU path correct (explicit device, bf16 autocast on train, no stray CPU tensors; fp32 inference intentional for calibrated probs). align joblib n_jobs=-1 sane for independent LOSO folds; BLAS-oversubscription is a known nuance, not changing unmeasured. Scan's 'already optimized' confirmed.

value: If a result looks too good vs SOTA/published, assume a bug/leak/eval-mismatch, not a breakthrough — verify under matched eval before claiming. Origin: fNIRS 0.442 'beat' BenchNIRS 0.389 was apples-to-oranges (LOSO+shrinkage vs 5-fold-group+plain-LDA); real matched result was a modest +3.7pp (0.429 vs reproduced 0.392).

Per-subject z-scoring is a real cross-subject transfer fix for EEG WORKLOAD band-power (the EEG analog of Riemannian re-centering for MI). Band-power is subject-idiosyncratic in ABSOLUTE scale; z-scoring each subject by its own feature mean/std (unsupervised, no labels) lifts EEG-bandpower->LDA from 0.407 raw to 0.511 (honest calibration-half) / 0.581 (transductive, uses test blocks' own stats). fNIRS does NOT benefit (0.474->0.448/0.483) - already per-epoch baseline-corrected so subject offset removed. So earlier 'EEG near-floor cross-subject regardless of feature' was WRONG - it was missing per-subject normalization. With it EEG (~0.51-0.58) >= fNIRS (~0.47), EEG is the stronger workload modality. IMPORTANT: transductive z (test-block stats) is ~7pt optimistic vs honest calib-half; report the calib-half ~0.51 as the defensible number.

pyriemann 0.11/0.12.dev = pure numpy (grepped installed source + docs + web: zero torch/array-api refs, deps numpy/scipy/sklearn/joblib). No GPU path without from-scratch torch reimpl of the geometry ops. Bench (numpy, BLAS-threaded): mean_riemann 22x22=36ms, 28x28=396ms, 72x72=5.9s for ~200 mats. Our riemann runs are 22-28 dim (bnci MI, eeg n-back) => already 36-400ms/fold, NOT a bottleneck; the 72-dim fnirs path uses fnirs_lda (features->LDA), never mean_riemann. Verdict: GPU-via-pyriemann = no win (no path + not the bottleneck + transfer overhead would dominate at these sizes). Deferred, do not wire.
